zh.lifeSummary: 當Joseph Wagner於1705年12月23日出生在德國薩阿藍德薩阿普法爾-可萊斯布利艾斯卡斯特爾比爾巴賀時，他的父親Johann Adam Wagner40歲，母親Anna Margaretha Kim38歲。他於1730年11月21日與Anna Maria Vogelgesang在德國貝也結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1746年9月14日在他的家鄉享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joseph Wagner于1705年12月23日出生在德国薩阿藍德薩阿普法爾-可萊斯布利艾斯卡斯特爾比爾巴賀时，他的父亲Johann Adam Wagner40岁，母亲Anna Margaretha Kim38岁。他于1730年11月21日与Anna Maria Vogelgesang在德国貝也结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1746年9月14日在他的家乡享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wagner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Wägner): occupational name for a carter and (in some dialects) a cartwright, from an agent derivative of Middle High German wagen ‘cart, wagon’, German Wagen. This surname is also established in many other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Britain, Poland, and Denmark. In Hungary it is mostly spelled Wágner and Vágner. In Russia, Czechia, Croatia, Slovenia, and Slovakia it is also found in the Slavicized form Vagner . Compare Wagener , Waggener , and Wagoner .

Dutch and perhaps also English: occupational name from Middle Dutch waghenaer ‘carter’ (compare 1 above). The Dutch word is not known to have been borrowed into English before 1600 but the surname Wagner is recorded in Norfolk (England) from 1379, perhaps a substitution of the Dutch word for Middle English wainer. Compare Waggoner .

variants.name: Waggener
meanings: Americanized form of German   Wagner   or its variant   Wagener  . Compare   Waggoner  .

variants.name: Wagler
meanings: German: variant of   Wagner  .

variants.name: Wanger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Franz, Aloysius, Frieda, Heinz, Kurt, Manfred.
German:
habitational name for someone from any of various places called Wang (Bavaria, Austria) or Wangen.

variants.name: Waggoner
meanings: Americanized form of German or Dutch   Wagner   or its German and Dutch variants   Wagener   and   Wagenaar  . Compare   Waggener   and   Wagoner  .
Possibly also English: altered form of Waghorn . The surname Waggoner is very rare in Britain.

History: A planter named John Waggener or Waggoner, who came to America c. 1670 and lived in Essex County, VA, is said to have been born in Colchester, Essex (England). No documentary source for this origin is given and an English origin is difficult to establish. The nearest similar English surname is Wagner 2, a very rare Norfolk name, originating in the 14th-century. In 17th-century Kent a number of families named Waghorn alias Wagon were occasionally also known as Waggoner (through false association with that word). Direct derivation from the English word wag(g)on is not possible, since it was borrowed into English only in the 16th century from Dutch, and wag(g)oner is an early Modern English coinage.

variants.name: Waner
meanings: Swabian habitational name for someone from a place called Wain, or from Wahn near Cologne.
possibly an altered form of Wähner, a variant of Wagner .
German (Wäner):

variants.name: Vagner
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic), Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian; Hungarian (Vágner); Slovak (also Vágner): occupational name for a carter or cartwright, of German origin (see Wagner ), often applied as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic occupational names or surnames.

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Kurt, Dieter, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Helmuth, Bernhard, Ernst, Hans, Alfons, Eldor.
German: northern and central variant of   Wagner  .

variants.name: Wagoner
meanings: Americanized form of German or Dutch   Wagner   or its German and Dutch variants   Wagener   and   Wagenaar  . Compare   Waggoner  .
Possibly also English: variant of Waggoner . The surname Wagoner is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Wagener
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Fritz, Arno, Bernhard, Dieter, Horst, Johann, Klaus, Kurt, Matthias, Willi.
German: variant of   Wagner  .

variants.name: Wegener
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Heinz, Friedrich, Johannes, Lorenz, Armin, Eldor, Erna, Guenther, Hans, Kurt, Manfred.
German, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Wagner  .

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Joseph Wagner was born on 23 December 1705, in Bierbach, Blieskastel, Saarpfalz-Kreis, Saarland, Germany, his father, Johann Adam Wagner, was 40 and his mother, Anna Margaretha Kim, was 38. He married Anna Maria Vogelgesang on 21 November 1730, in Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 14 September 1746, in his hometown, at the age of 40.

fr.Bierbach, Blieskastel, Saar-Pfalz-Kreis,: Bierbach, Blieskastel, Saarpfalz-Kreis, La Sarre, Allemagne
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joseph Wagner est né le 23 décembre 1705, à Bierbach, Blieskastel, Saarpfalz-Kreis, La Sarre, Allemagne, son père, Johann Adam Wagner, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Anna Margaretha Kim, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Anna Maria Vogelgesang le 21 novembre 1730, à Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 14 septembre 1746, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joseph Wagner am 23. Dezember 1705 in Bierbach, Blieskastel, Saarpfalz-Kreis, Saarland, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Johann Adam Wagner 40 und seine Mutter Anna Margaretha Kim 38. Er hat am 21. November 1730 in Bayern, Deutschland Anna Maria Vogelgesang geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 14. September 1746 im Alter von 40 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joseph Wagner è nato il il 23 dicembre 1705 nel Bierbach, Blieskastel, Saarpfalz-Kreis, Saarland, Germania, il suo padre, Johann Adam Wagner, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Anna Margaretha Kim, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Anna Maria Vogelgesang il 21 novembre 1730, nel Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 14 settembre 1746, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 40 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Joseph Wagner(이)가 1705년 12월 23일 독일 사알랜드 사아르p팔즈-크레이스 블리스카스텔 비르바치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Johann Adam Wagner(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Margaretha Kim(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1730년 11월 21일 독일 베이른에서 Anna Maria Vogelgesang(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1746년 9월 14일 그의 고향에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.23 December 1705–14 September 1746: 23 de dezembro de 1705–14 de setembro de 1746
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joseph Wagner nasceu em 23 de dezembro de 1705, em Bierbach, Blieskastel, Saarpfalz-Kreis, Saarland, Alemanha, seu pai, Johann Adam Wagner, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Anna Margaretha Kim, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Anna Maria Vogelgesang em 21 de novembro de 1730, em Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 14 de setembro de 1746, em sua cidade natal, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joseph Wagner родился 23 декабря 1705 г. в местности биирбач, блиескастел, саарпфалз-крис, саарланд, Германия, его отцу по имени Johann Adam Wagner было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Margaretha Kim, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Maria Vogelgesang 21 ноября 1730 г. в местности беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 14 сентября 1746 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 40 лет.

es.23 December 1705–14 September 1746: 23 de diciembre de 1705–14 de septiembre de 1746
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joseph Wagner nació el 23 de diciembre de 1705, en Bierbach, Blieskastel, Saarpfalz-Kreis, Saarland, Alemania, su papá, Johann Adam Wagner, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Anna Margaretha Kim, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Anna Maria Vogelgesang el 21 de noviembre de 1730, en Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 14 de septiembre de 1746, en su pueblo, a la edad de 40 años.
